title: Family Dinner

jokes.text: Oh my God, I guess I was saving it for during your run, then.
jokes.setup: Alexis complains David ate yogurt she was saving for after her run
jokes.punchline: David sarcastically suggests he was saving it for during her run instead
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical sarcasm that fits David's character perfectly - defensive wit with a logical twist

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, I was saving that yogurt for after my run.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry. I didn't realize you were saving it for during your run instead.

jokes.text: Agh, you're like a big, dirty raccoon, David!
jokes.setup: David has been eating her food
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to a raccoon - specific and unexpected animal choice
jokes.explanation: Vivid, unexpected imagery that's both specific and character-appropriate for Alexis

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, you're like a raccoon. You go through the garbage, you have those little hands, and you're always wearing the same outfit.

jokes.text: Oh Alexis, you're almost forty, if you want some food, prepare yourself some food!
jokes.setup: Alexis complaining about food situation
jokes.punchline: David points out her age while being equally helpless himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's hypocritical nature and sibling dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I can't believe we're out of food again. This is like, literally the worst thing that's ever happened to me.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're 28 years old, Alexis. And I'm standing here in a silk robe at 2 PM with no idea how to make a sandwich.

jokes.text: Nobody can cook, this is basically child abuse!
jokes.setup: Family unable to cook for themselves
jokes.punchline: Dramatically escalating to 'child abuse' for privileged adults
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis dramatic escalation - privileged person calling inconvenience 'abuse'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't know how to cook.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: None of us know how to cook.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We've never had to cook.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: This is literally child abuse.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, we're all adults.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Emotionally, I'm twelve.

jokes.text: That was thrown out after Alexis left her extensions on it, and everything smelled like burnt hair.
jokes.setup: Reference to the Teppanyaki table
jokes.punchline: Specific, gross visual of burnt hair extensions destroying expensive equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific visual that's both gross and perfectly character-specific for Alexis

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not using a Teppanyaki table. Do you know what happens when you put hair extensions near an open flame? It's a fire hazard. And expensive.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh please, David. I've been doing this for years without incident.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, well, your hair extensions melting onto my equipment and destroying it would be more than an incident. It would be a catastrophe.

jokes.text: I thought it was an actual table, David.
jokes.setup: David's story about her ruining the cooking equipment
jokes.punchline: Her oblivious explanation delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis obliviousness - missing the obvious while sounding reasonable

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You ruined the cooking equipment.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I was trying to make a smoothie and I may have put the blender in the dishwasher.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You put the blender in the dishwasher?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, it needed to be washed.

jokes.text: There was a plug coming out of the side of it!
jokes.setup: Alexis's oblivious defense
jokes.punchline: David's exasperated point about the obvious electrical nature
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing David's exasperation with Alexis's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, it's a lamp. It requires electricity to function. That's not a personality trait, that's basic physics.

jokes.text: Excuse me, but the enchiladas were my mother's recipe.
jokes.setup: Family reminiscing about their former cook's dishes
jokes.punchline: Moira's prideful correction claiming ownership of servant's cooking
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira pride - taking credit for something that wasn't hers

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, actually, those were my recipes. I had them prepared.

jokes.text: I made it for you all the time!
jokes.setup: Johnny's skeptical response to her claim
jokes.punchline: Moira doubling down on clearly false claim
jokes.explanation: Moira's defensive escalation is in character but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm not being defensive, I'm being factual.

jokes.text: if that's the way you remember things, sweetheart, that's...
jokes.setup: Moira's insistent false memory
jokes.punchline: Johnny's diplomatic non-confrontation trailing off
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's conflict avoidance with Moira

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You were there, Johnny. You remember. We were at that gala, and you wore that—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I... I don't think I was at a gala.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Of course you were! And you said to the ambassador—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm, well... that's... that's possible, I suppose. I mean, we've been to many... events, and I'm sure at some point I wore... something, so...

jokes.text: Oh my gosh David, it's gonna be so fun for you tonight, doing that with Mom.
jokes.setup: Moira volunteering David to help cook
jokes.punchline: Alexis's fake enthusiasm about David's unwanted assignment
jokes.explanation: Typical sibling schadenfreude delivered with Alexis's trademark false sweetness

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, David, that's so great! You're going to be cooking? That's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I didn't volunteer for this.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, I know, but still. It's going to be so fun. You're going to be amazing at it.

jokes.text: Don't touch me! Don't! That's harassment!
jokes.setup: Alexis touching him while being fake-nice
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic escalation to 'harassment' over sibling touch
jokes.explanation: Perfect David dramatic overreaction to normal sibling interaction

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh David, you look so handsome today.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm just going to touch your arm for a second.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Please don't.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh, come on. It's just a little touch.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's harassment. I'm calling the police. This is assault. You've crossed a line, Alexis. A very clear line.

jokes.text: Listen, a small favour... I require the use of your kitchen.
jokes.setup: Moira approaching neighbor for help
jokes.punchline: Calling demanding someone's kitchen a 'small favour'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira obliviousness to what constitutes a reasonable request

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I need to ask you a small favour.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Can I use your kitchen?

jokes.text: Of course I'd say... yes, it's just that that darn stove is on the fritz.
jokes.setup: Moira asking to use kitchen
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's hesitant agreement immediately followed by excuse
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jocelyn's reluctance to say no directly

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: May I use your kitchen?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, um, yes, of course.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Although, I'm about to use it myself.

jokes.text: Because this pie is scorching! / It comes and goes, Roland!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn claiming stove is broken
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately contradicting with evidence, Jocelyn's weak excuse
jokes.explanation: Well-executed setup/payoff with Roland unwittingly exposing Jocelyn's lie

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: The stove is broken.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It's not broken. I used it this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Well, it wasn't working when I tried to use it.

jokes.text: Who'd you call, honey? Bill?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn claiming she called a repairman
jokes.punchline: Roland innocently asking for details that expose the lie
jokes.explanation: Roland's oblivious continuation that makes Jocelyn's situation worse.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: A repairman? Which company? I know all the repairmen in town.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, um... it was... a local one.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What was the name of the company?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I... I don't remember.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, what did he fix?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: The... the thing. You know.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Jocelyn, I've lived here my whole life. There's only three repairmen, and I play poker with all of them. None of them came to your house.

jokes.text: Bill doesn't do stoves. / Then, Gary. / Gary? Our dentist??
jokes.setup: Jocelyn claiming she called repair service
jokes.punchline: Roland exposing each lie, culminating in calling the dentist for stove repair
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity with Roland systematically destroying each excuse

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I called the repair service this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Jocelyn, you called the dentist.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Well, he's very handy.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: He's a dentist. You asked him to fix the stove.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: He said he'd think about it.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Of course he did, because you called the dentist about your stove.

jokes.text: as long as you make extra for us, and leave a ten spot over there for the hydro.
jokes.setup: Finally allowing Moira to use kitchen
jokes.punchline: Immediately adding conditions including payment for electricity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland character moment - helping but with petty conditions

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Alright Moira, you can use the kitchen.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: But you're gonna have to pay me for the electricity.

jokes.text: Which isn't so much a rush as much as it is just a... A handful of people showing up at a leisurely pace.
jokes.setup: Twyla mentioning the 'lunch rush'
jokes.punchline: Her honest description of how unbusy the cafe really is
jokes.explanation: Good understated humor showing Twyla's honest assessment of business

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, during the lunch rush, we get maybe two, three people coming in. And that's if Ronnie stops by for coffee. Last Tuesday, a tumbleweed actually rolled through the cafe. I'm not exaggerating. It was real.

jokes.text: I am just gonna put these plates down. I just think I'll think better, once the blood rushes from my arms, back to my head.
jokes.setup: Johnny giving long-winded speech while Twyla holds heavy plates
jokes.punchline: Twyla's polite but pointed way of asking to put down heavy plates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy with Twyla's characteristically polite way of handling Johnny's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know, Twyla, I've been thinking about the state of the restaurant industry, and how it's evolved over the years. The customer experience, the ambiance, the presentation of our dishes—it all speaks to who we are as proprietors. When I opened this establishment, I had a vision, and that vision was...

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Mr. Rose, these plates are getting really hot.

jokes.text: Totally, they're just heavy plates!
jokes.setup: Johnny misunderstanding her plate comment as agreement to his pitch
jokes.punchline: Twyla's simple clarification about the literal weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect miscommunication comedy - Johnny's business talk vs. Twyla's practical concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So you're on board? This is great!

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: No, I was just saying the plate is heavy.

jokes.text: If you had anything to offer.
jokes.setup: Bob saying Johnny would do the same for him
jokes.punchline: Johnny's brutal honest assessment of Bob's lack of offerings
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical putdown delivered with Johnny's oblivious honesty

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, let's be honest, what would I do for you? I mean, what do you have that I would want? Your coupons? Your business sense? Your charm? Your hair?

jokes.text: Oh, okay, it's just 'cause the last time I was in here, you told me to leave your office, so, I guess I was just having a hard time gauging where we're at, as two people who used to um... be well, um...
jokes.setup: Alexis asking if it's safe to be in Ted's office
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt to define their past relationship
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing post-breakup awkwardness and Alexis's discomfort with directness

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Is it safe for me to be in here? I don't want to make things weird.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, we dated for years. I think we're past the point of things being weird.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, yes, we were... we were together. In a romantic... capacity. For an extended... time period.

jokes.text: I don't like you anymore. I mean, like that.
jokes.setup: Ted claiming to be 'over it'
jokes.punchline: His clarification that accidentally sounds even worse
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection where the clarification makes it worse - perfect awkward timing

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'm over it. I'm completely over it.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: What I meant to say is, I'm over her. I'm not over the fact that she's with someone else.

jokes.text: Are you wearing makeup? / So what if I am?
jokes.setup: Post-breakup conversation
jokes.punchline: Ted's defensive reaction to being called out for clearly caring about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ted trying too hard to look good post-breakup

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You're clearly obsessed with how you look.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I am not obsessed with how I look. I just... take pride in my appearance.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You spent forty minutes on your hair this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That's called grooming, Alexis. It's what adults do.

jokes.text: Oh my God, it's like we're sharing a brain!
jokes.setup: Ted suggesting they take time before re-proposing
jokes.punchline: Alexis's enthusiastic agreement to something she actually doesn't want
jokes.explanation: Good irony as Alexis agrees enthusiastically to the opposite of what she wants

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Maybe we should take some time before we think about getting engaged again.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes! That's such a good idea. I think that's really smart. Yeah, let's definitely do that.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: No! / 'Cause you and me... / You and I... / No, uh-huh, uh-huh, uh-huh! It's-mmm!
jokes.setup: Ted asking if she had something else to say
jokes.punchline: Alexis's complete verbal breakdown trying not to break up with him
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy showing Alexis literally unable to form words when put on the spot

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Um, I... Well, you see, the thing is... I think that maybe... possibly... we should probably... I mean, not that there's anything wrong with you, because you're like, super great, and your hair is really... it's doing a thing. But I just... I can't... We can't... I'm sorry, I just need to say this and I'm going to say it but I'm also not going to say it, but I am. Ted, I think we should break up. No, wait, that came out wrong. What I meant was... I think we need to take some time. Actually, no. I think... I know that this is really hard to hear, but I have to be honest with you and with myself, and the honest truth is that I don't think this is working anymore, and I'm really sorry, and you're amazing, but I'm just not... I can't... I'm breaking up with you. I'm so sorry.

jokes.text: Bob, you told me you had a sweet little office space, okay, and frankly, working out of a garage is not what I had in mind.
jokes.setup: Bob showing Johnny the 'office space'
jokes.punchline: Johnny's polite but firm rejection of the obviously inadequate space
jokes.explanation: Standard fish-out-of-water humor with Johnny's expectations vs reality

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: So this is your office space.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I see. Well, it's... it's very compact.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's cozy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's a closet, Bob.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: It's a storage room with potential.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: There is no potential here. There's barely room to breathe. I can't conduct business in a closet.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, where else would you like to work?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Anywhere but here would be preferable, Bob.

jokes.text: An office! You know, a chair, for starters!
jokes.setup: Bob asking what Johnny had in mind
jokes.punchline: Johnny's exasperated listing of basic office requirements
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Johnny's frustrated expectations

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A desk. A chair. Perhaps a filing cabinet. Maybe some pens. A telephone would be nice. A computer of some kind. Some paper. Possibly some tape. A stapler. Some sort of organizational system. And maybe, just maybe, a window.

jokes.text: I don't know if it was the timbre of his voice, or the fact that he smelled like baby powder, but I just, I physically couldn't do it!
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why she couldn't break up with Ted
jokes.punchline: Specific, unexpected details about his voice and baby powder smell
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific details that are both funny and reveal Alexis's particular attractions

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I couldn't break up with him because every time I tried, he'd start talking about his cases, and his voice gets really deep and authoritative, and then he smells like baby powder, and I just... I couldn't do it.

jokes.text: Like, there must be people out there who say yes to getting married because they can't say no, and then what happens?
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her inability to break up with Ted
jokes.punchline: Her realization about people who marry by default
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about people trapped by their inability to say no.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just realized something. People who marry by default are basically just giving up. Like, they're settling for someone because they're afraid of being alone, or because it's easier than actually looking for love.

jokes.text: Well, I'm pretty sure those people get divorced.
jokes.setup: Alexis wondering what happens to people who can't say no to marriage
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact response about divorce
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - logical, blunt, and delivered with characteristic dryness.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What happens to people who can't say no to marriage?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: They get divorced.

jokes.text: You know, it's interesting, the longer we have this conversation, the more over it I get.
jokes.setup: Alexis questioning how quickly he got over their relationship
jokes.punchline: Ted's sarcastic observation about getting more over it during the conversation
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcastic comeback with perfect timing and genuine wit

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You got over me pretty quickly.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I'm getting over it more as we speak.

jokes.text: In fact, I'm having a hard time remembering when I wasn't over it.
jokes.setup: Stevie claiming to be getting more over Patrick
jokes.punchline: Escalation to claiming she was always over it
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Stevie's defensive mechanism - rewriting history.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm definitely over Patrick. Like, completely over him.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Actually, I was over him the moment he left. It was instant.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: In fact, I was over him before he even left. I was already over it.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I've been over him this whole time.

jokes.text: What does 'fold in the cheese' mean? / You fold it in. / I understand that, but how?
jokes.setup: David asking for cooking clarification
jokes.punchline: Moira's completely unhelpful repetition of the same phrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that became a cultural reference - perfect setup/payoff with escalating frustration and quotable dialogue

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How long should I cook this for?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Until it's cooked, David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, but how long is that?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Until it's cooked.

jokes.text: Okay, I don't know how to fold broken cheese like that! / Then I don't know how to be any clearer!
jokes.setup: Continuing fold in the cheese argument
jokes.punchline: Both characters' frustrated responses showing complete communication breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the fold-in-cheese bit with both characters at peak frustration

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I folded it the way one folds cheese. It's not complicated.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, you don't fold cheese. You slice it. Or you crumble it. You certainly don't fold it like it's a letter to the Queen.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you had actually explained what you wanted instead of just standing there with that expression on your face—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This expression is my resting face, David. It's not a instruction manual.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's more of a warning label.

jokes.text: You just fold it in.
jokes.setup: David asking for specific instructions on folding cheese
jokes.punchline: Moira's unhelpful repetition of the same phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the escalating 'fold in the cheese' sequence - Moira's complete unhelpfulness.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How do you fold cheese?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You fold it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, but how?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You just fold it.

jokes.text: If you say 'fold in' one more time... / It says, fold it in! / This is your recipe! / You fold in the cheese, then! / Don't you dare! / You fold it in!
jokes.setup: Peak of fold in the cheese argument
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire escalation with threats and repeated phrase
jokes.explanation: Climax of the iconic 'fold in the cheese' sequence - perfectly escalated absurdity

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You folded the cheese!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I folded it to preserve its structural integrity!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You don't fold cheese, Moira!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I'm folding it again!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Don't you dare fold that cheese!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm folding it into a swan!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You fold that cheese and we are done!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We are already done, David. We've been done!

jokes.text: If you say 'fold in' one more time...
jokes.setup: Continued back and forth about folding cheese
jokes.punchline: David's threat about hearing 'fold in' again
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation building to the climax of the cooking disaster.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: If I hear the words 'fold in' one more time, I'm going to lose it.

jokes.text: David! What does burning smell like?!
jokes.setup: Cooking disaster occurring during argument
jokes.punchline: Moira's panicked question revealing her complete cooking incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character moment - Moira doesn't even know what burning smells like

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Why is it smoking?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Is that supposed to happen?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: How do I turn it off?

jokes.text: Well, I was about to give Hazel a blood test. / Well, I think it's probably better if you put Hazel down. / What?! No, she'll be fine!
jokes.setup: Ted about to give animal medical care
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding about 'put down' meaning euthanasia vs set down
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with dark humor that gets resolved quickly and logically

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to put down the cat now.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, what? You're going to put down Mr. Whiskers?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yes, I'm going to put down the cat so I can examine him.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ted, no! You can't just put down Mr. Whiskers!

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, I need to set the cat down on the table to check his vitals.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ohhh... okay, that makes so much more sense.

jokes.text: You're the perfect boyfriend, Ted. / Thank you... / But... / I knew there was a 'but.'
jokes.setup: Alexis giving Ted a compliment
jokes.punchline: Ted's weary recognition of the inevitable 'but'
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Ted's experience with breakup conversations

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know, Ted, you're actually really smart.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Thank you?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: But...

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: There it is.

jokes.text: No, no, no! There is no 'but.' / But you're just not the perfect boyfriend for me.
jokes.setup: Alexis insisting there's no 'but'
jokes.punchline: Immediately following with exactly that 'but'
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with Alexis contradicting herself within seconds

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: There's no 'but.'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: But...

jokes.text: Yeah, no, I pieced that part together.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining she can't accept his proposal
jokes.punchline: Ted's dry acknowledgment of the obvious conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response showing Ted's intelligence and resignation

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I can't accept your proposal.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah, I gathered that.

jokes.text: Well, I did book us a two week couples vacation that's non-refundable. / Ooh, yeah, that makes... That makes it harder.
jokes.setup: Breakup conversation winding down
jokes.punchline: Ted's mention of non-refundable vacation complicating things
jokes.explanation: Good escalation adding practical complications to emotional situation

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: And I already paid for that couples' retreat in Turks and Caicos. Non-refundable.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, we're breaking up.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I know, but it's non-refundable.

jokes.text: They're Japanese graphic novels. It's called Manga. / Oh, well, that sounds like a... Lucrative career choice.
jokes.setup: Eric correcting Bob about his art
jokes.punchline: Johnny's sarcastic assessment of manga as career choice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's business-minded dismissiveness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm looking at manga.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I thought I might take it up as a career.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, you're fifty-five.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, the perfect age to start drawing Japanese cartoons.

jokes.text: Either does Johnny, and he's not even related to me.
jokes.setup: Eric saying he has no money
jokes.punchline: Bob's brutal honesty about Johnny's financial situation and relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bob moment - brutally practical and inadvertently insulting

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, you know, Johnny, I hate to break it to you, but you're broke. You have no money. And frankly, the fact that you're still married is a miracle.

jokes.text: And uh, just so you know, garage opens at eight, but if you're here a few minutes before that, even better!
jokes.setup: Johnny agreeing to take the garage space
jokes.punchline: Bob immediately treating Johnny like an employee with opening duties
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob character moment - exploiting Johnny's desperation immediately

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Okay, so you're gonna be here at 6 AM to open up. You'll need to unlock the front gate, flip the sign, turn on all the lights, and get the coffee going.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: 6 AM? I thought we were just sharing the space.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, now you're sharing it as an employee. And employees open.

jokes.text: or start a car without a key. / Then I met your father, and suddenly I had people to do everything for me.
jokes.setup: Moira listing her former practical skills
jokes.punchline: Revealing how wealth made her helpless
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing how privilege created helplessness

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was once very practical. I could change a tire, make a soufflé, and I knew the difference between a bidet and a... well, I still don't know the difference between a bidet and a douche.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: But that was before the money. Now I can't do any of those things. I can't even open a jar without calling someone.

jokes.text: And now I know how it feels to be utterly helpless, like you, and your sister. / I don't know if I'd call us 'helpless.' / No need to make me feel better.
jokes.setup: Moira comparing herself to her children
jokes.punchline: Her dismissal of David's mild objection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira obliviousness - insulting her children while being self-pitying

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm not like you and your sister. I have a certain je ne sais quoi.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, that's not really—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's French, David. I wouldn't expect you to understand.

jokes.text: like you, and your sister.
jokes.setup: Moira saying she knows how it feels to be utterly helpless
jokes.punchline: Her casual comparison of her children to helpless people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - brutally honest assessment of her children delivered matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I know exactly how it feels to be utterly helpless. I have three children.

jokes.text: Well, that's simple enough, any fool could do that. / Did you do that?! / No.
jokes.setup: Moira watching cooking video and declaring it simple
jokes.punchline: David's pointed question and her admission she didn't do it
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with David calling out Moira's hypocrisy efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, this looks absolutely simple. I could do this in my sleep.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Did you do it?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: And it's rare to find someone that beautiful, and smart, and deeply selfish, yet charming!
jokes.setup: Ted describing what made Alexis special
jokes.punchline: Including 'deeply selfish' in the list of positive qualities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Ted's honest assessment of Alexis that's both loving and accurate

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: She was incredibly intelligent, and funny, and beautiful, and deeply selfish.

jokes.text: Not bad. Not bad, you two! Not bad at all!
jokes.setup: Family trying the clearly terrible enchiladas
jokes.punchline: Johnny's repeated, overly diplomatic assessment
jokes.explanation: Good diplomatic Johnny moment trying to be supportive of obviously bad food

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: They're... they're very... they're something.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I mean, you can really taste the... effort.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And that's... that's what's important.

jokes.text: It's creamy. Very creamy.
jokes.setup: Family trying the disastrous enchiladas
jokes.punchline: Alexis's diplomatic description of the texture
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the cooking disaster - polite way to describe something awful.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's like eating a tire fire, but like, in a good way.

jokes.text: Although it's really not that complicated, once you fold in the cheese!
jokes.setup: Moira suggesting they could cook regularly
jokes.punchline: Her casual reference to the phrase that caused the earlier disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the iconic 'fold in the cheese' moment with Moira's oblivious confidence

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We could cook regularly. It's not that hard. I mean, how difficult can it be to just... fold in the cheese?

jokes.text: We had a lengthy conversation about hosiery and menopause. / Again, that was me.
jokes.setup: Previous revelation about breakfast confusion
jokes.punchline: Even more specific conversation that Moira had with David thinking he was Alexis
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and episode closer - absurd specific details with David's deadpan correction

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was telling David about my morning, and he looked at me like I had three heads.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because you were describing a conversation you had with Alexis, but you kept saying 'you.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, how was I supposed to know? You were both standing there!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We were not both standing there. I was in the back room for twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A detail I was unaware of at the time.

tmdb_overview: Moira decides to cook dinner with the help of David. Johnny scopes out an office for his consulting business. And Alexis finally comes clean with Ted about their engagement.
insight: Escalation-driven character comedy sustains 86.5 score across 57 jokes in 23 minutes.
